scenario.prompt: It's free project time! You want to...

options.text: Write a fun story or poem
Draw a big colorful picture
Make up a silly song about it
Build a model out of blocks or clay

scenario.prompt: You have 12 crayons to share with 3 friends. You...

options.text: Count them out carefully so everyone gets the same
Ask everyone which colors they want most
Think quietly about the fairest way
Sort them by color like sorting leaves by type

scenario.prompt: Your class is making a treasure map. You want to...

options.text: Draw all the paths and landmarks on it
Add symbols that feel magical and meaningful
Make it on a computer or tablet
Write all the clues and riddles

scenario.prompt: At recess, what sounds most fun to you?

options.text: Making up songs or clapping rhythms with friends
Counting how many times you can jump rope
Doing cartwheels and relay races
Playing with your best friend all day

scenario.prompt: Your best kind of break from school is...

options.text: Running around or playing a sport
Sitting quietly and daydreaming
Looking for bugs, flowers, or clouds outside
Wondering about something mysterious

scenario.prompt: A new kid at school looks lonely. You...

options.text: Walk over and ask them to play with you
Show them the cool things you can do on the school iPad
Leave them a friendly note on their desk
Think about the best way to include them in the group

scenario.prompt: When something upsets you, you feel better by...

options.text: Thinking it through quietly by yourself
Drawing or painting how you feel
Humming a song that matches your mood
Running around or playing outside

scenario.prompt: Outside at recess, you love to...

options.text: Look closely at bugs, plants, and animals
Play games with lots of friends
Wonder why the sky is blue or where clouds come from
Take photos of things with a tablet
Why people are alive and what happens when they die
How to describe your feelings in a perfect poem
How numbers explain patterns in everything around us
How to draw a picture that shows something impossible
A cool video or app you made on a device
A song you wrote and want to perform
Your special journal of drawings and thoughts
A collection of cool things you found in nature

likert.statement: I love reading books and stories.
I am good at explaining things using words.
I enjoy writing stories, poems, or letters.
I notice when someone uses a cool or interesting word.
I love solving math problems.
I like finding patterns in things.
I like having clear steps to follow.
I enjoy puzzles and brain teasers.
I can picture things in my head really well.
I love drawing and making pictures.
I know where I am and don't get lost easily.
I like to draw pictures when I am trying to figure something out.
I can remember a song after only hearing it a few times.
Music makes me feel different emotions.
I often hum or tap a beat without thinking about it.
I can tell when something sounds wrong in a song.
I learn best by doing things with my hands.
I love sports, dancing, or building things.
It is hard for me to sit still for a long time.
I am good at sports and moving my body.
I like working with other kids more than working alone.
I can tell when my friend is sad even if they don't say so.
My friends come to me when they need help or feel sad.
I am good at helping groups work together.
I think a lot about who I am and what I like.
I like to work by myself sometimes.
I know what I am good at and what I need to work on.
I pay attention to how I am feeling inside.
I notice plants, animals, and bugs that others miss.
I feel happy and calm when I am outside in nature.
I like learning about different animals and plants.
I care a lot about taking care of nature.
I wonder why people are alive and what life means.
I think about big questions like fairness and being good.
I like talking about deep ideas.
I think about what it means to be a good person.
I learn new apps and games really fast.
I love making things on a computer or tablet.
I understand how computers and devices work.
I am good at finding things online.
